'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]]
==歴史== この神社は、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[1839年]]（[[天保]]10年）平沼九兵衛が祭ったのに始まるとされる。
==所在地== *神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20

==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]]

{{神社| 名称=水天宮平沼神社| 位置=| 所在地=神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20| 創建=[[1839年]]（[[天保]]10年）| 社格=| 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]| 本殿=| 例祭=9月5日| 画像=}}
'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の、[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]・西平沼町・[[高島町]]・[[みなとみらい]]・緑町の[[氏神]]。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]]
==歴史== この神社は、[[江戸時代]]後期の[[1839年]]（[[天保]]10年）平沼九兵衛が祭ったのに始まるとされる。当初は[[水天宮]]であったが、[[明治政府]]の[[社格]]制度により、平沼の[[村社]]として改称し平沼神社となる。しかし、その後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬されていた為、[[横浜市]]の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも水天宮と表記されていた。その為、現在では[[氏子]]・崇敬者から水天宮平沼神社と称されている。
==所在地== *神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]

revision.comment: 座標追加。ついでに公式ｻｲﾄをもとに加筆
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=水天宮平沼神社| 画像=| 所在地=神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]| 社格=旧村社| 創建=[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）| 本殿=| 例祭=9月5日| }}
'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の、[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]・西平沼町・[[高島町]]・[[みなとみらい]]・緑町の[[氏神]]。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。
==歴史== [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛が祭ったのに始まるとされる。社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後]][[久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる帷子川（[[今井川]]）に架かる元平沼橋付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくうひらぬましんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:西区 (横浜市)]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=水天宮平沼神社| 画像=[[File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|240px]]| 所在地=神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]| 社格=旧村社| 創建=1839年| 本殿=| 例祭=9月5日| 神事 =湯の花神事| }}
'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の、[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]・西平沼町・[[高島町]]・[[みなとみらい]]・緑町の[[氏神]]。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。
==歴史== [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祭ったのに始まるとされる。
社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後]][[久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる元平沼橋付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[相模鉄道]][[平沼橋駅]]徒歩1分
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]

revision.comment: 情報箱内の記述は和暦でいいと思います。

当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる元平沼橋付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。

==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日

revision.comment: 社名ついての記述は、詳細が「歴史」の中に有り、簡素化の為、削除いたしました。又、交通手段を追加しました。

なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。

==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。

社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる元平沼橋付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[相鉄本線]][[平沼橋駅]]徒歩1分 *[[京急本線]][[戸部駅]]徒歩5分

'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の、[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]・西平沼町・[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]]・[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]・緑町の[[氏神]]である。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。
==歴史== [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祀ったのに始まるとされる。
社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる元平沼橋付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[相鉄本線]][[平沼橋駅]]徒歩1分 *[[京急本線]][[戸部駅]]徒歩5分
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]

==歴史== [[ファイル:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|かつては本沼橋（現在の元平沼橋）のたもと（画像右端）に祀られていた（歌川貞秀「横浜名所一覧」より、1860年頃）]] [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祀ったのに始まるとされる。
社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる[[平沼橋|元平沼橋]]付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町）
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[相鉄本線]][[平沼橋駅]]徒歩1分 *[[京急本線]][[戸部駅]]徒歩5分
==備考== *[[神紋]]は「縄付き錨」。 *御朱印対応は[[2013年]]（平成25年）以降行っていない。
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]

{{神社| 名称=平沼神社| 画像=[[File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|240px]]| 所在地=神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=水天宮平沼神社}}| 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]]| 社格=旧村社| 創建=天保10年（1839年）| 本殿=| 別名=水天宮平沼神社| 例祭=9月5日| 神事 =湯の花神事| }}
'''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。旧平沼新田鎮守。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の[[氏神]]である。
==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。

当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる[[平沼橋|元平沼橋]]付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町）
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[相鉄本線]][[平沼橋駅]]徒歩1分 *[[京急本線]][[戸部駅]]徒歩5分

==歴史== [[ファイル:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|かつては本沼橋（現在の元平沼橋）のたもと（画像右端）に祀られていた（歌川貞秀「横浜名所一覧」より、1860年頃）]] [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祀ったのに始まるとされる。
社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米市|久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。
当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる[[平沼橋|元平沼橋]]付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。
なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。
==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日
==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町）
==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[平沼橋駅]]（相鉄）徒歩1分 *[[戸部駅]]（京急）徒歩5分
==備考== *[[神紋]]は「縄付き錨」。 *御朱印対応は[[2013年]]（平成25年）以降行っていない。
==関連項目== *[[水天宮]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=平沼神社 | 画像=[[File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | 所在地=[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]]平沼2-8-20 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=水天宮平沼神社}} | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]] | 社格=旧[[村社]] | 創建=[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]） | 本殿= | 別名=水天宮平沼神社 | 例祭=9月5日 | 神事 =湯の花神事 }} '''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。旧平沼新田鎮守。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の[[氏神]]である。 ==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。 ==歴史== (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。 なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。 ==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。 ==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日 *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日 ==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町） ==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[平沼橋駅]]（相鉄）徒歩1分 *[[戸部駅]]（京急）徒歩5分 ==備考== *[[神紋]]は「縄付き錨」。 revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | Image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923年}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863年}}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013年}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | Image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923年}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863年}}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913年}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013年}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]]{{Myōken Faith}}[[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year }}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year }} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year }}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year }}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year }}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year }} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]]{{Myōken Faith}}[[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year }}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year }}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year }} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | Image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|12=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=draft}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|12=draft}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|12=draft}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|12=draft}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=draft}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=draft}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|12=draft}}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|12=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|12=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|12=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|12=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|12=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|12=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|12=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|12=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|12=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|12=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|12=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|12=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|12=draft}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=draft}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|12=draft}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|12=draft}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|12=draft}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=draft}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=draft}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|12=draft}}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|12=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|12=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|12=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|12=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|12=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|12=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|12=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|12=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|12=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|12=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|12=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|12=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|安徳天皇|" to "|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|"
revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|12=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|12=draft|lt=Nishi Ward}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|12=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|12=draft}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|12=draft}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=draft}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|12=draft}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|12=draft}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=draft}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|12=draft}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=draft}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=draft}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|12=draft}}). Under the modern shrine ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|12=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|12=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|12=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|12=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|12=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|12=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|12=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|12=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|12=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|12=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|12=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|12=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|12=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|12=draft|lt=Nishi Ward}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=draft}}. revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|明治|" to "|明治|1=Meiji era|"
28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:水天宮平沼神社 up to rev 101736428
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=平沼神社 | 画像=[[File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | 所在地=[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]]平沼2-8-20 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=水天宮平沼神社}} | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]] | 社格=旧[[村社]] | 創建=[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]） | 本殿= | 別名=水天宮平沼神社 | 例祭=9月5日 | 神事 =湯の花神事 }} '''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。旧平沼新田鎮守。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の[[氏神]]である。 ==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。 ==歴史== [[ファイル:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|かつては本沼橋（現在の元平沼橋）のたもと（画像右端）に祀られていた（歌川貞秀「横浜名所一覧」より、1860年頃）]] [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祀ったのに始まるとされる。 社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米市|久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。 当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる[[平沼橋|元平沼橋]]付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。 なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。 ==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。 ==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日（[[屋台]][[営業]]や[[神幸祭|神輿渡御]]や[[神楽|神楽殿]]での催し物なども行われる） *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日 ==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町） ==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[平沼橋駅]]（相鉄）徒歩1分 *[[戸部駅]]（京急）徒歩5分 ==備考== *[[神紋]]は「縄付き錨」。 *御朱印対応は[[2013年]]（平成25年）以降行っていない。 ==関連項目== *[[水天宮]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくうひらぬましんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:横浜市西区の歴史]] [[Category:横浜市西区の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[水天宮平沼神社]] to [[Suitengū Hiranuma Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=平沼神社 | 画像=[[File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | 所在地=[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]]平沼2-8-20 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=水天宮平沼神社}} | 祭神=[[天之御中主神]]・[[安徳天皇]] | 社格=旧[[村社]] | 創建=[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]） | 本殿= | 別名=水天宮平沼神社 | 例祭=9月5日 | 神事 =湯の花神事 }} '''水天宮平沼神社'''（すいてんぐうひらぬまじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[西区 (横浜市)|西区]][[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]]にある神社である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。旧平沼新田鎮守。[[横浜駅]]東口周辺の[[氏神]]である。 ==祭神== *[[天御中主神]] *[[安徳天皇]] 安徳天皇は[[水天宮]]の神で、安産と水の神とされるが、特に[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）の[[関東大震災]]で崩壊を見なかっただけでなく、近隣で発した火災でも類焼を免れた事と、[[昭和]]20年（[[1945年]]）5月29日の横浜大空襲で横浜市内が焦土と化した中で、鎮座地の一角だけは焼け落ちなかった事から、火難除けとして崇められている。 ==歴史== [[ファイル:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|かつては本沼橋（現在の元平沼橋）のたもと（画像右端）に祀られていた（歌川貞秀「横浜名所一覧」より、1860年頃）]] [[江戸時代]]後期の[[天保]]10年（[[1839年]]）、平沼九兵衛（5代目）が祀ったのに始まるとされる。 社伝によれば、当時の平沼一帯は[[塩田]]であり、塩田作業中の村人が入江に流れついた[[祠]]を見つけ、これを沖へ返そうとする度に岸に戻ってくるので、平沼九兵衛にその事を伝えると、九兵衛が[[守護神]]の無いこの地に祀れとの啓示であろうと、岸に上げて平沼新田の守護神として祀るようになったといい、また祠の中に[[筑後国|筑後]][[久留米市|久留米]]の[[水天宮 (久留米市)|水天宮]]の神札が祀られていたため、「水天宮」と称して、安産と水の神として広く崇敬されたという。 当初は現在の横浜市西区西平沼町を流れる[[帷子川]]（今井川）に架かる[[平沼橋|元平沼橋]]付近に鎮座していたが、その後の発展とともに鎮座地を何度か転じ、現在地への遷座は[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）である。[[明治政府]]の[[近代社格制度|社格制度]]により、平沼の村社として正式名称を「平沼神社」と改称し、[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に[[本殿]]の前に[[拝殿]]を新築、現在の社殿構成となった。 なお社名は、初めが「水天宮」であったため、[[明治]]の改正により「平沼神社」と改称した後も横浜の水天宮として崇敬され、横浜市の公開する戦前・戦後の市内地図にも「水天宮」と表記され、現在でも[[氏子]]・崇敬者からは「水天宮平沼神社」と称されている。 ==末社== 境内に平沼稲荷神社、稲荷神社、竈三柱神社、平沼天満宮がある。 ==祭事・年中行事== *例祭：9月5日（[[屋台]][[営業]]や[[神幸祭|神輿渡御]]や[[神楽|神楽殿]]での催し物なども行われる） *湯の花神事（湯立神事）：1月5日 ==氏子地域== *西区[[平沼 (横浜市)|平沼]] *西区西平沼町 *西区[[高島 (横浜市)|高島]] *西区緑町 *西区[[横浜みなとみらい21|みなとみらい]]（一部。旧緑町） ==所在地・交通== 神奈川県横浜市西区平沼2-8-20 *[[平沼橋駅]]（相鉄）徒歩1分 *[[戸部駅]]（京急）徒歩5分 ==備考== *[[神紋]]は「縄付き錨」。 *御朱印対応は[[2013年]]（平成25年）以降行っていない。 ==関連項目== *[[水天宮]] ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html 水天宮平沼神社サイト] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すいてんくうひらぬましんしや}} [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:水天宮]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:横浜市西区の歴史]] [[Category:横浜市西区の建築物]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|qq=draft|lt=Nishi Ward|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Shinto Yok}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|qq=draft|lt=Nishi Ward|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
