Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa]] to [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|qq=draft|lt=Nishi Ward|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Ward|qq=}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Antoku Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Antoku|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|qq=draft|lt=Nishi Ward|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku|qq=}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Station|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Antoku Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Antoku|qq=}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu|qq=}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Salt Fields|qq=}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small Shrine|qq=}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu (Kurume)|qq=}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo Province|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume City|qq=}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1863|qq=}}). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|qq=}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Food Stalls|qq=}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Procession|qq=}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagura|qq=}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Mirai 21|qq=}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge Station|qq=}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobe Station|qq=}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2013|qq=}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yokohama Shrines]] [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}}{{ill|Nishi Ward|ja|西区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi Ward|qq=}} Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Antoku Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Antoku|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}}) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}}{{ill|Nishi-ku|ja|西区|qq=draft|lt=Nishi Ward|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku|qq=}} {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of {{ill|Yokohama Station|ja|横浜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Station|qq=}}. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Antoku Tennō|ja|安徳天皇|1=Emperor Antoku|2=ja|3=安徳天皇|4=de|5=Antoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Antoku Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Antoku|qq=}} Antoku Tennō is the deity of {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu|qq=}}, and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}}) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} in the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 10 ({{ill|1839|ja|1839年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1839|qq=}}), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its {{ill|Salt Fields|ja|塩田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Salt Fields|qq=}}, and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine {{ill|Small Shrine|ja|祠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Small Shrine|qq=}} that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the {{ill|Suitengu (Kurume)|ja|水天宮 (久留米市)|lt=Suitengu|lt_ja=水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu (Kurume)|qq=}} of {{ill|Chikugo Province|ja|筑後国|lt=Chikugo|lt_ja=筑後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikugo Province|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurume City|ja|久留米市|lt=Kurume|lt_ja=久留米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurume City|qq=}}) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|1863|ja|1863rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1863|qq=}}). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}}) a new worship hall ({{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}}) was constructed in front of the main hall ({{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|qq=}}), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring {{ill|Food Stalls|ja|屋台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Food Stalls|qq=}}, {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [{{ill|Divine Procession|ja|神幸祭|lt=Mikoshi Procession|lt_ja=神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Procession|qq=}}] as well as events held in the {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|lt=Kagura Hall|lt_ja=神楽殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagura|qq=}}) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Minato Mirai 21|ja|横浜みなとみらい21|lt=Minato Mirai|lt_ja=みなとみらい|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato Mirai 21|qq=}} (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge Station|ja|平沼橋駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge Station|qq=}} (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * {{ill|Tobe Station|ja|戸部駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobe Station|qq=}} (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since {{ill|2013|ja|2013rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2013|qq=}} (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * {{ill|Suitengu|ja|水天宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu|qq=}} ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:横浜市西区の建築物]] [[Category:横浜市西区の歴史]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:横浜市西区の歴史]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama�]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yokohama Nishi Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]]

formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suitengu]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

{{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = {{ill|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|ja|Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|qq=}} | Annual Festival = September 5 |image=Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG | Shinto Ritual = Yunohana Ritual }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|qq=}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|qq=}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|qq=}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|qq=}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Yunohana Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|qq=}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]
