formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}}

[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}}

Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}}

[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. According to the shrine legend, the Hiranuma area at that time was known for its [[:en:Salt evaporation pond|Salt Fields]], and villagers working in the salt fields discovered a small shrine [[:en:Hokora|Small Shrine]] that had drifted into the cove. Every time they attempted to return it to the sea, it would float back to the shore. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden. Moreover, because a deity tablet from the [[:en:Kurume Suitengū|Suitengu]] of [[:en:Chikugo Province|Chikugo]] ([[:en:Kurume|Kurume]]) was enshrined within the small shrine, it came to be known as “Suitengu” and was widely revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Originally, the shrine was enshrined near the {{ill|Kazamaki River|ja|帷子川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazamaki River|1=Katabira River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481286}} (now known as Imai River) that flows through what is now Nishihiranuma-cho in Nishi Ward, Yokohama, near the bridge {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|lt=Former Hiranuma Bridge|lt_ja=元平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}}. With subsequent development, the location of the shrine was relocated several times, with the current relocation taking place in [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 3 ([[:en:1863|1863]]). Under the modern shrine Ranking system of the {{ill|Meiji Government|ja|明治政府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Government|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治維新|1=Meiji restoration|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8707}}, it was officially renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” as the village shrine of Hiranuma, and in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]) a new worship hall ([[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]]) was constructed in front of the main hall ([[:en:Honden|Main Hall]]), forming the current shrine complex. Although the shrine was originally named “Suitengu,” and was later renamed “Hiranuma Shrine” under [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] reforms, it continued to be venerated as Yokohama’s Suitengu. On both prewar and postwar city maps published by Yokohama, it is still labeled “Suitengu,” and even today parishioners and devotees refer to it as “Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine.” ==Subsidiary Shrines== Within the precincts are Hiranuma Inari Shrine, Inari Shrine, Kamameshihara Shrine, and Hiranuma Tenmangu. ==Festivals and Annual Events== * Annual Festival: September 5 (featuring [[:en:Market stall|Food Stalls]], {{ill|Vendor Operations|ja|営業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vendor Operations|1=business activity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q19862406}}, and a mikoshi procession [[[:en:Shinko-shiki|Mikoshi Procession]]] as well as events held in the [[:en:Kagura|Kagura Hall]]) * Sinter Ritual (Yunotachi Ritual): January 5 ==Parishioner Areas== * Nishi Ward: {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}} * Nishi Ward, Nishi-Hiranuma Town * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Takashima|Takashima]] * Nishi Ward, Midorimachi * Nishi Ward: [[:en:Minato Mirai 21|Minato Mirai]] (partly; formerly Midorimachi) ==Location and Access== Kanagawa Prefecture, Yokohama City, Nishi Ward, Hiranuma 2-8-20 * [[:en:Hiranumabashi Station|Hiranuma Bridge Station]] (Sotetsu) – 1 minute on foot * [[:en:Tobe Station|Tobe Station]] (Keikyu) – 5 minutes on foot ==Notes== * The shrine mon is a “chain-attached anchor.” * The issuance of goshuin (shrine stamps) has not been available since [[:en:2013|2013]] (Heisei 25). ==Related Articles== * [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]] ==External Links== * [http://www.hiranuma-suitengu.or.jp/index.html {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja|水天宮平沼神社サイト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine Website|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] {{Shinto stub}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|水天宮平沼神社|version=101736428|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548437}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Nishi-ku, Yokohama]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yokohama]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Suiten-gū Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] [[ja:水天宮平沼神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11548437]] Wikidata name: Suitengu Hiranuma Jinja Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Source citation|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiranuma Shrine | image = File:Suitenguu Hiranuma Jinjya.JPG|250px
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E}}}} | Location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] Hiranuma 2-8-20 | Coordinates = {{Wiki coordinates|35|27|31.2|N|139|36|57.7|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ・ [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] | Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Founded = [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]) | Main Hall = | Alternative Name = {{ill|Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|ja|水天宮平沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|1=Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548437}} | Annual Festival = September 5 | Shinto Ritual = Sinter Ritual | location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1203393}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.458667|N|139.616028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} }} '''Suitengu Hiranuma Shrine''' (水天宮平沼神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]][[:en:Nishi-ku|Nishi Ward]] {{ill|Hiranuma|ja|平沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma|1=Hiranuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1311638}}. Its former shrine Rank was {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is also known as the former guardian shrine of Hiranuma Shinden and serves as the ujigami for the area around the east exit of [[:en:Yokohama Station|Yokohama Station]]. ==Enshrined Deities== * {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}} * [[:en:Emperor Antoku|Emperor Antoku]] Antoku Tennō is the deity of [[:en:Suitengū|Suitengu]], and is revered as a god of easy childbirth and water. Not only did he remain intact during the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 ([[:en:1923|1923]]) {{ill|Kantō Great Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Great Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, but he was also spared from conflagration during a nearby fire, and even amid the scorched-earth devastation of Yokohama during the Yokohama air raid on May 29, [[:en:1945|1945]] in the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era, one corner of the shrine’s precincts remained unburned. For these reasons, he is venerated as a protector against fire disasters. ==History== [[File:Hon-numaBashi1860.jpg|thumb|Once enshrined at the base of Hon-numa Bridge (present-day {{ill|Hiranuma Bridge|ja|平沼橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranuma Bridge|1=Hiranuma Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120139}} as Former Hiranuma Bridge) (from Utagawa Sadahide’s “List of Yokohama Landmarks”, circa 1860)]] In the latter part of the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 10 ([[:en:1839|1839]]), Hiranuma Kyūbee (the 5th generation) is said to have first enshrined the deity. When they reported this phenomenon to Kyūbee, he interpreted it as a divine revelation to enshrine it in this land that lacked a {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|1=Guardian deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131278957}}, and so he raised it to the shore and established it as the guardian deity of Hiranuma Shinden.
